problem.title: K'th Smallest/Largest
problem.description: Find the kth smallest or largest element in an unsorted array. QuickSelect achieves average O(n) by partitioning around a pivot and recursively searching only the relevant partition, avoiding a full sort. The key insight is that after partitioning, the pivot is at its final sorted position, so we only need to recurse into one side.
assumptions: Find kth smallest (or largest with similar approach).

example.explanation: Sorted: [3,4,7,10,15,20]. 3rd smallest = 7. Use QuickSelect or heap.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – QuickSelect
solutions.description: QuickSelect works like QuickSort but only recurses into the partition containing the target index, giving O(n) average time. After each partition step the pivot lands at its final sorted position, so we compare that position to k and discard the irrelevant half. The key insight is that finding the kth smallest is equivalent to finding the element at index k in a sorted array without fully sorting it.
